mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds a 3% year-over-year threshold, it signals potential central bank tightening or erosion of purchasing power, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation often precedes interest rate hikes
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and low unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and economic health. This environment fosters corporate profitability and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint condition of strong retail sales and low unemployment consistently leads to negative S&P 500 60-day returns over any 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase the cost of capital and reduce liquidity, which negatively impacts corporate valuations and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Rising rates typically depress equity valuations
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are predominantly positive during periods of sustained FEDFUNDS increases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening demand in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: If a >10% MoM decline in HOUST is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports overall economic expansion and translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output is a key component of GDP
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and contracting GDP indicates a deteriorating economic environment, signaling a recessionary period that typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND GDPC1 YoY growth < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are characterized by high unemployment and negative GDP growth
failure_condition: If the joint condition of rising unemployment and low GDP growth consistently leads to positive S&P 500 60-day returns over any 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical threshold (e.g., 4%), it signals a tight labor market that can lead to wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a very short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Very low unemployment can signal overheating economy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 4%, the hypothesis is rejected.
